name: Jowain ibn Malik
title: Defector from Falsehood
shortDescription: Someone who came to Karbala with Umar Sa'd's army but, upon seeing their betrayal, joined the Imam and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Juwayn ibn Malik al-Duba'i (or al-Taymi) belonged to the Bani Taym tribe and lived in Kufa. Initially, he was dispatched to Karbala with Ibn Ziyad's forces under Umar ibn Sa'd's command.

However, Juwayn had Shia inclinations within himself. When he saw that Ibn Sa'd rejected all peaceful proposals from Imam Hussain (AS) and definitively intended to shed the Prophet's grandson's blood, his conscience wouldn't let him rest.

Along with some of his tribesmen, he defected from Ibn Sa'd's army in the dark of night, joined Imam Hussain's (AS) radiant camp, and repented.

On Ashura, during the first attack (the enemy's shower of arrows), Juwayn ibn Malik fought manfully against his former tribesmen and attained the great grace of martyrdom.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى جُوَيْنِ بْنِ مالِكٍ الضُّبَعِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Juwayn ibn Malik al-Duba'i...
